sections.paragraph:
πάροδος, ου, ἡ (s. prec. entry; Thu. et al.; ins, pap, LXX; PsSol 11:5; EpArist; Philo, Praem. 112; Joseph.).

① a way for travelers, passage, thoroughfare (X., An. 4, 7, 4 al.; Diod S 20, 23, 2; EpArist 118; Jos., Ant. 14, 46) πάροδός ἐστε τῶν … you are the highway for those … IEph 12:2. ἡ πάροδος μετὰ τῶν ἀγγέλων the way to the angels Hv 2, 2, 7 (s. Joly’s note); Hs 9, 25, 2.

② the act of moving to a point of reference and continuing on one’s course, but with possibility of a stopover, passing by ἐν π. (Thu. 1, 126, 11; Polyb. 5, 68, 8; Cicero, Ad Att. 5, 20, 2; Lucian, D. Deor. 24, 2; PSI 354, 8; PLond III, 1041, 2 [VII a.d.]; Jos., Ant. 14, 38) ἰδεῖν τινα see someone in passing 1 Cor 16:7.—DELG s.v. ὁδός. M-M.

prec. prec. = preceding

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

PsSol PsSol = Psalms of Solomon—List 2

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5
  2. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

Joly Joly = Hermas, Le Pasteur; s. under H—List 1

Hs Hs = Similitudes

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Att. Att. = Attic, esp. in ref. to dialect

Lucian Lucian , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

PSI PSI = Papiri greci e latini: Pubblicazioni della Società Italiana—List 4

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 779
